steps.title: Identify the structure of the underlined sentence
steps.content: The underlined sentence reads: "Yawn contagion occurs when one individual yawns in response to another's yawn." This has the form of a definition — it names a phenomenon and explains what it is. The rest of the passage then discusses research findings about this defined phenomenon.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — It defines a phenomenon that is discussed in the text.: The sentence names and defines yawn contagion, which the rest of the passage explores through research on primates, wild populations, and social patterns. ✓
  • Choice B — It introduces a problem that is examined in the text.: The sentence does not present a problem — it states what yawn contagion is. No difficulty or issue is raised. ✗
  • Choice C — It makes a claim that is challenged in the text.: The passage does not challenge the definition — it builds on it. Nothing in the text disputes that yawn contagion occurs. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents a hypothesis that is evaluated in the text.: A hypothesis is an untested prediction. The sentence states an established definition, not a conjecture to be tested. ✗
A is correct.
